data.title: Samir Arora says war risk a 1% tail event, can’t position for that; India theme strong for foreign investors
data.description:
The odds of a full-scale war between India and Pakistan remain extremely low—just about 1%—and not something investors can predict or position for, says Samir Arora, founder of Helios Capital. Instead, he argues, the bigger story is India’s resilience and long-term strength as a global investment destination, despite temporary geopolitical flare-ups.

“A war situation and big collapse is a 1% probability event which we cannot predict,” Arora said. “But for this India-Pakistan tension, the India theme is only getting stronger.” At this point, you have to believe heart-of-heart that the situation will remain contained and this will ease sooner than later, he said.

Even as tensions flared on Wednesday, Arora noted that markets are behaving predictably, with a modest decline that reflects concern but not panic going by indication from Gift Nifty. “A 1% kind of fall on a day like this is a given. Over the weekend, we’ll have to watch whether things get better or worse,” he said.

Quick reversal likely once dust settles

Even if there is a further escalation and markets fall further, Arora expects Indian equities to rebound sharply when the war stops. “The day this stops, markets will come back with a vengeance, recovering at least 80% of the loss. That is what history tells us,” he said.

Currently, the situation remains controlled. “There has been only some sort of remote targeting, and things do not seem out of control,” he said. “India’s stance is still one of controlled response and de-escalation. Even though we can clearly see India claiming victory, the key for this to end will be for Pakistan to also claim a victory—without that, this will not stop.”

Foreign flows point to a structural shift

Remarkably, despite the geopolitical noise, foreign investors were net buyers on Wednesday. “The world has been overweight US for the longest time—nearly two decades,” Arora said. “Even if the tariff issues are somewhat resolved, global investors will not go overweight U.S. again. There’s now a belief that you need to de-risk from the US.”

He noted that since the global financial crisis in 2008, around $20 trillion has flowed into U.S. equities. “Even if $5 trillion gets pulled out now, it would mean massive inflows into global markets—and India is a very strong contender to attract a meaningful share of that.”In the past two years, the Indian market was largely propped up by local investors, while foreigners were consistent sellers. “Now, if foreign investors simply stop selling—and in fact start buying—there won’t be any stopping the market,” Arora said.

data.title: Toray Industries Unit Toray Advanced Materials Unveils Snolene Cooling Fabric; Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Toray Industries subsidiary Toray Advanced Materials developed a new cooling fabric called Snolene, using proprietary technology, according to a company release on Friday.

The product rapidly disperses heat upon skin contact through its high thermal conductivity, lowering perceived body temperature by 3-6 degrees Celsius, Pulse News said in a Thursday report.

Shares of Toray Industries rose nearly 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: UBS Discussing Sale of Hedge Fund Unit to Cantor Fitzgerald
data.description:
UBS Group is discussing the sale of its hedge fund unit O'Connor with a potential revenue-sharing agreement with Cantor Fitzgerald, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The news outlet noted that the Swiss bank has been exploring ways to divest riskier businesses and fulfill extra capital requirements amid stricter regulation.

UBS did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Financial Stocks Rebound, Materials Stocks Slump
data.description:
The financial sector rebounded 1.2% at midday Friday. Shares on Wall Street rallied after the US and the UK officials announced a trade framework that will see tariffs on British cars, steel, and aluminum imported to the US lowered or removed.

Macquarie Group reported fiscal 2025 earnings of AU$9.755 per diluted share, up from AU$9.114 per diluted share a year earlier. The board declared a dividend of AU$3.90, compared with AU$3.85 in the prior reporting period, payable on July 2 to shareholders of record as of May 20.

Meanwhile, the materials sector struggled, falling 0.8%. Iron ore futures fell overnight after China's top steel association confirmed that output curbs were underway.

Chrysos entered into a master services agreement with Newmont for Newmont to use its PhotonAssay technology for its gold mining projects. The companies also entered into a contract for an initial PhotonAssay unit to be installed at its Ahafo mine in Ghana, with deployment expected in the first half of 2026.

data.title: China's April exports beat expectations, imports narrow declines
data.description:
China's exports expanded 8.1% year-on-year in April, while imports contracted 0.2%, customs data showed on Friday, both defying expectations for a much sharper slowdown in trade.

Economists polled by Reuters had forecast a 1.9% increase in exports and a 5.9% drop in imports.

The new data followed a 12.4% year-on-year jump in exports in March, when Chinese factories pushed out shipments before U.S. President Donald Trump's 145% tariffs on Chinese goods took effect on April 9. Imports had fallen 4.3% in March.

China has retaliated against U.S. tariffs by ramping up its levies on U.S. imports to 125%. The tit-for-tat trade war is threatening China's exports, which had been a lone bright spot in the country's patchy post-pandemic economic recovery.

Officials from the two countries are meeting this weekend in Switzerland to start trade negotiations.

data.title: Market Chatter: Microsoft Bars Deepseek Use Over Data, Propaganda Fears
data.description:
Microsoft has barred employees from using AI tools developed by Chinese startup Deepseek, citing data security risks and concerns over propaganda, Reuters reported Thursday, citing President Brad Smith.

Smith said Microsoft also excludes Deepseek's app from its store, warning that user data could be sent to China and the tool might generate content aligned with Beijing's messaging, according to the report.

Disclaimer

data.title: China's Export Growth Remained Resilient in April
data.description:
(03:02 GMT) *China Apr Exports +9.3% on Year in Yuan Terms
(03:02 GMT) *China Apr Imports +0.8% on Year in Yuan Terms
(03:06 GMT) *China Apr Exports +8.1% on Year; Expected +2.5%
(03:07 GMT) *China Apr Imports -0.2% on Year; Expected -5.5%
(03:07 GMT) *China Apr Trade Surplus $96.18B; Expected Surplus $97.6B
(03:08 GMT) *China Apr Trade Surplus CNY690B
(03:19 GMT) China's Export Growth Remained Resilient in April

China's exports continued to expand at a solid pace in April, despite hefty U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods, official data showed Friday.

Outbound shipments rose 8.1% from a year earlier in April, after rising 12.4% in March, the General Administration of Customs said Friday. The result beat the 2.5% increase expected by economists in a Wall Street Journal poll.

Imports fell 0.2% from a year ago in April, narrowing from the 4.3% decline in March, official data showed. That was also better than the 5.5% fall expected by the surveyed economists.

That put China's trade surplus at $96.18 billion in April, compared with $102.64 billion in March and the $97.6 billion tipped by the economists.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Hong Kong Forex Reserves Slip in April
data.description:
Hong Kong's foreign currency reserves slipped to $408.7 billion in April from $412.5 billion as of the end of March, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority said Thursday.

The amount is five times the currency in circulation or equivalent to 36% of the city's dollar M3 money supply, the de facto central bank said.

Including unsettled forex contracts, Hong Kong's foreign currency reserve assets were at $407.9 billion in April compared with $411. 9 billion in March.

data.title: KSB Bags New Export Order From German Parent
data.description:
KSB has secured an export order of an undisclosed amount from its parent Company KSB SE & Co. KGaA, Germany, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

KSB will supply nuclear pumps, including eight critical safety class 2 pumps, for two units of the parent's newly constructed nuclear power plant in Europe.

data.title: DataDot Technology Unit Signs Agreement With Australian Insurer
data.description:
DataDot Technology said its unit, DataDot Technology, has entered into a pilot agreement with an Australian insurer, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The agreement enables the use of its PropertyVAULT portal and services to enhance the identification and recovery of stolen assets.

The agreement is effective immediately and will operate through to April 2026.

The company said it is in "positive and progressive" discussions with other major insurers to implement the PropertyVAULT insurance services offering.

data.title: China Trade Surplus Beats Estimates
data.description:
China's trade surplus jumped to USD 96.18 billion in April 2025, up from USD 72.04 billion in the same period a year earlier, exceeding market expectations of USD 89 billion.

The sharp increase was largely boosted by an 8.1% year-on-year rise in exports, much faster than market forecasts of a 1.9% gain.

Meanwhile, imports unexpectedly grew by 0.8%, easily beating market expectations of a 5.9% fall, reversing a 4.3% drop in March.

data.title: Market Chatter: Putin and Xi Deepen Russia-China Strategic Ties
data.description:
Russia and China seek to deepen their strategic partnership, with presidents Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping signing a joint statement on Thursday, Reuters reported on the same day.

Putin said Russia-China relations were self-sustaining and that the two nations plan to expand the use of their national currencies in mutual settlements, according to the report.

data.title: Jameson Resources Says Review of Canada Coal Project Bankable Feasibility Study Endorses Validity; Shares Rise 33%
data.description:
Jameson Resources said that independent technical advisers reviewed its bankable feasibility study for the Crown Mountain hard coking coal project in British Columbia, Canada, and the competent person for the study remains valid based on the review of the updated information, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Since the release of the study in 2020, there have been inflationary increases in both capital and operating costs in Canada as well as in competitor producer locations, the filing said.

The review identified the range of capital and operating costs increases based on the repricing of key capital equipment items and the adoption of published increases to labor, fuel, construction, and infrastructure costs.

The outcomes from the review using the updated economic inputs resulted in an estimated increase of 28% to the project's pre-production capital from $309 million to $394 million, as well as a 15% increase in cash operating costs from $89.41 per tonne in the yield optimization study to $102.79 per tonne.

The review also considered the impact of revised coal price forecasts and exchange rates.

The firm's shares jumped over 33% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Enel Reports Higher Q1 Attributable Profit, Total Revenue
data.description:
Enel (ENEL.MI) said Thursday its profit and revenue increased year over year in the first quarter.

Profit attributable to owners of the parent for the three months ended March 31 was 2.01 billion euros, compared with 1.93 billion euros earlier. EPS was unchanged at 0.19 euro.

The Italian energy company's total revenue was 22.07 billion euros, compared with 19.43 billion euros a year ago.

For full-year 2025, the group expects net ordinary income between 6.7 billion euros and 6.9 billion euros. For 2027, net ordinary income is expected to be in the range of 7.1 billion euros to 7.5 billion euros.

data.title: Locksley Resources Files Drilling Application for US Project; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Locksley Resources submitted the drill permit application for the Desert Antimony Mine, within its Mojave project in California, to the US Bureau of Land Management, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Sampling at the mine yielded "exceptional" results, including 46% antimony from rock chip assays, with eight samples above 17% antimony, and mineralization mapped across a 400-meter surface strike length, the filing said.

The company plans to conduct further exploration in high-grade antimony and rare earth element zones through surface and subsurface methods, per the filing.

Shares were up 5% in afternoon trade Friday.

News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Gain After Trump Touts U.K. Deal, High Hopes on China

The Dow industrials climb 0.6%; Nasdaq adds 1.1%.

Trump's Bespoke Trade Deal With U.K. Sets Little Precedent for Other Nations

Many other agreements weren't seen as likely to come together quickly.

Bitcoin rallies with stocks to top $100,000 again as exuberance returns to markets

Bitcoin rose above $100,000 for the first time since February, reigniting hopes that the crypto may climb to a new record high.

With U.K. Deal, U.S. Signals That 10% Tariff on World Is New Baseline

Both countries are hailing this as a good outcome, showing how much the landscape has shifted.

RBA to Walk Rate-Cut Path With Dangers on Both Sides

The Reserve Bank of Australia is set to cut rates and keep on cutting until the official cash rate is at a far less restrictive level.

Trump's Tariffs Turned a Sleepy Industry Into the Front Lines of the Trade War

For years, customs brokers have helped companies calculate tariffs and expedite imports. The trade war has turned their business into a game of whack-a-mole.

New York Fed Survey Finds Consumers Ambivalent on Inflation Outlook

Consumers sent mixed signals about their inflation expectations in the New York Fed's April survey. But the poll showed no serious rise in long-term inflation expectations.

Canada's Financial Stability Faces Stiff Test From U.S. Tariffs, Bank of Canada Says

Annual central bank report says there is a risk permanent tariffs could cause high unemployment and business insolvencies.

Why the Fed Isn't Ready to Join Other Central Banks in Cutting Rates

The U.S. faces risks of higher inflation and unemployment. Countries without tax hikes on imports are focused on the hit to growth.

EU Targets American Aircraft, Car Parts for Possible Tariffs if Talks Fail

The bloc said it could target more than $100 billion worth of American products with tariffs if negotiations with the U.S. break down.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Lyft Swings to Profit Amid Increase in Ridership

San Francisco-based company said rides grew 16% year over year, to 218.4 million, ahead of analyst views for 215.1 million.

McKesson to Spin Off Medical-Surgical Company

The pharmaceutical distributor said the new company would be a differentiated medical surgical supply and solutions business.

CoStar Agrees to Buy Australian Advertiser Domain for $1.9 Billion

U.S. real-estate analytics provider CoStar agreed to buy property advertiser Domain in a sweetened deal.

News Corp Revenue Boosted by Gains at Dow Jones, HarperCollins

Digital and print Wall Street Journal subscriptions totaled 4.34 million for the March quarter, the company says.

Paramount Notches Streaming Growth, but Revenue Drops

The company saw gains in its streaming business, but its overall revenue declined compared with the same period last year when it televised the Super Bowl.

Peloton Slashes Marketing Again in Hard Play for Profitability

Double-digit percentage cuts continue for the fourth consecutive quarter as the fitness company's marketing chief leaves.

Coinbase Reports Drop in Profit

Earlier in the day Coinbase announced a $2.9 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire Deribit, a bitcoin options exchange.

TKO Group Raises Full-Year Outlook on Recent Acquisitions, Swings to Profit

The new forecasts came as TKO Group swung to a profit and posted higher-than-expected revenue in the recent quarter.

Air Canada Loss Widens, Full-Year Outlook Lowered on Economic Uncertainty

Chief Executive Michael Rousseau said he expects market conditions to be unsteady moving forward as the economic outlook appears uncertain

Funko Pulls Outlook as Loss Widens, Sales Fall

The company said it accelerated plans to diversify its supply chain, especially with regard to imports from China.

data.title: Fraser & Neave's Attributable Profit Rises Marginally in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Fraser & Neave's attributable profit to shareholders rose 0.3% to SG$84.1 million in the six months ended March 31, from SG$83.2 million a year earlier, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share after exceptional items declined slightly to SG$0.057 from SG$0.058 in the comparable period.

Revenue was up 13% year over year to SG$1.21 billion from SG$10.7 billion, due to strong sales form the beverages segment.

data.title: Umm Al Qaiwain General Investments' Q1 Net Profit, Total Revenue Rise
data.description:
Umm Al Qaiwain General Investments (ADX:QIC) said Thursday that its net profit and total revenue for the first quarter rose year over year.

Net profit after tax for the three months ended March 31 was 12.2 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 11 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.034 dirham from 0.030 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed investment company's total revenue was 13 million dirhams, compared with 12.7 million dirhams earlier.
